metadata.title: Send the Middle of the Network, Not the Picture

beats.narration_text: Talofa, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The image is twenty-seven kilobytes and the radio can't carry it — but layer six's output is eight kilobytes and doesn't even look like a picture.

props.command: My device can't transmit full images to the server. What can I send instead?
output: Run the first layers on-device. Ship intermediate activations — smaller than the input, full-model accuracy, partial privacy.

beats.narration_text: The book's MobileNetV2 split-inference diagram: activation tensor sizes annotated at each layer boundary. Find the cut where activations are smaller than your radio budget.

A 224 by 224 RGB image. That's 150,528 bytes — about 147 kilobytes. Every pixel, three channels. The radio pipe is narrow. At the bandwidth your sensor has, this takes more than a second to transmit. Multiply that by your inference rate and the math doesn't close.

Watch what happens inside the network. Layer one: 112 by 112 by 64 — still large. Layer two: 56 by 56 by 128. Each conv-pool pair halves the spatial dimensions. By layer six: seven by seven by 512. That's 25,088 bytes — twenty-four kilobytes. Six times smaller than the original image. The network compressed the signal before you had to.

The cut. Left of the dashed line runs on-device — six convolutional layers, a few hundred milliseconds. Right of the line runs on the server — the rest of the network, full model accuracy. What crosses the line is 24 kilobytes of activations, not 147 kilobytes of pixels. Six times less bandwidth. The image never left the device. That's the privacy bonus.

Your turn. Ask Claude: My model has these layer output sizes — and here you list sizes in KB per layer. My radio bandwidth is open bracket bps close bracket bits per second. Walk me through: one — where the activation tensor is smallest, the split point. Two — latency to transmit from device vs transmitting raw input. Three — which early layers I must run on-device. Four — privacy implications of shipping intermediate activations vs raw input.

props.command: My model has these layer output sizes: [list of sizes in KB per layer]. My radio bandwidth is [bps] bits/second. Walk me through: (1) where the activation tensor is smallest (the split point), (2) latency to transmit from device vs transmitting raw input, (3) which early layers I must run on-device, (4) privacy implications of shipping intermediate activations vs raw input.
output: Split at the narrowest activation. 224×224×3 = 147 KB → 7×7×512 = 24 KB. 6× bandwidth savings. Raw pixels never leave the device.

props.title: Send the Middle of the Network, Not the Picture
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
